title: Scaling Rule of <mark>Electrochemical Impedance</mark> Spectroscopy for Nanoscale Ion Transport Pathways
authors: Yu Kambe, Paul F. Nealey and Zhongyang Wang
abstract: Nanoscale defects within polymer electrolytes disrupt ion transport pathways, resulting in variations in <mark>ionic conductivity</mark>. Quantifying <mark>electrochemical impedance</mark> spectroscopy (EIS) signal from connected and disconnected ionic pathways remains challenging without knowing information of all nanoscale transport pathways in polymer electrolytes. In this work, devices with precisely patterned nanoscale defects in ion conduction pathways have been fabricated to quantify the impact of pathway connectivity on ion transport. Each device is designed with a distinct ratio of connected to disconnected pa…
